channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Buckingham Palace clarifies King Charles' stance following Trump remarks on Iran nuclear weapons
item.description: During King Charles III and Queen Camilla’s state visit to the United States, President Donald Trump suggested at a formal White House dinner that the King agreed with his position that Iran should no...

item.title: Bannu police foils armed attack, terrorist killed and weapons recovered
item.description: Early Wednesday morning, Bannu police successfully repelled a violent attack on the Mazanga police post, resulting in the death of one terrorist and injuries to others. According to police officials, ...

item.title: CGBSE Class 10 and 12 Results 2026: Access and Scorecard Updates
item.description: The Chhattisgarh Board of Secondary Education (CGBSE) has announced that the Class 10 and Class 12 results for the year 2026 are now officially released. Students trying to access the results from the...

item.title: Guinness Record Holder Antoine Moses to Spearhead 24-Hour Mangrove Planting Drive in Mombasa
item.description: Kenya is set to host a major environmental event as Guinness World Record holder Antoine Moses leads a 24-hour mangrove restoration drive at Mirarani Site in Tudor Creek, Mombasa County. The exercise,...

item.title: Society for Planet and Prosperity calls for increased focus on state-level climate governance

item.title: Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya Class 9 Lateral Entry Results Announced
item.description: The Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya (JNV) Class 9 lateral entry examination results have been officially declared. Students who appeared for the test can now check their scores online. The lateral entry sc...

item.title: The Decline of Empires: Lessons from Rome and America
item.description: The article discusses the decline of empires, drawing parallels between ancient Rome and modern-day America. It highlights the reign of Roman Emperor Commodus, who took power as a teenager and indulge...

item.title: Experts weigh in on Australia’s new scheme requiring tech giants to pay for local news
item.description: The Albanese government has introduced the 'news bargaining incentive', a plan aimed at making major tech platforms compensate Australian publishers for the news content shared on their platforms. Whi...

item.title: PM Holds Talks with EU Business Delegation to Strengthen Trade Ties
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif received a high-level European Union delegation along with top executives from prominent European companies at the Prime Minister House on Tuesday. The delegat...

item.title: Oilers win 4-1 over Ducks to stay alive in playoff series
item.description: The Edmonton Oilers avoided elimination in their first-round playoff series against the Anaheim Ducks with a decisive 4-1 victory in Edmonton. Leon Draisaitl led the way, scoring two goals, including ...

item.title: Australian Designers Creating Custom Looks for International Pop Stars
item.description: Several young Australian designers are making a global mark by creating bespoke costumes for the biggest names in pop music. Caroline Reznik, a graduate of the University of Technology Sydney, designe...

item.title: Lawmakers Urged to Maintain Formality in Vice President Impeachment Hearing

item.title: Kaizer Chiefs look to continue unbeaten run against Siwelele FC
item.description: Kaizer Chiefs are set to face Siwelele FC at the Free State Stadium in a Betway Premiership fixture on Wednesday evening, hoping to secure three points and maintain their strong form. The club is curr...

item.title: Calgary Stampeders Select Eric Rascoe and Matt Sibley in 2026 CFL Draft
item.description: In the 2026 Canadian Football League (CFL) Draft, the Calgary Stampeders have strengthened their roster by selecting two notable players, Eric Rascoe and Matt Sibley. Rascoe, known for his versatile s...

item.title: Abubakar Sokoto Mohammed Foundation to honour late scholar with book launch and symposium
item.description: Friends, colleagues, and admirers of the late Dr Abubakar Sokoto Mohammed dey gather for one special event for Abuja today. The Abubakar Sokoto Mohammed Foundation don organize one symposium plus book...

item.title: Fifa enables Afghan women’s team in exile to compete internationally
item.description: Fifa has introduced a rule change allowing Afghan women footballers, who were forced into exile following the Taliban's return to power in 2021, to participate in official international matches. The T...

item.title: PSG Beat Bayern Munich 5-4 in First Leg of Champions League Semi-Finals
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain and Bayern Munich delivered an electrifying match in the first leg of the Champions League semi-finals on April 28, ending 5-4 in favour of PSG. The game at Parc des Princes was a ...

item.title: Arsenal Gains Premier League Edge After Manchester City's Fixture Request Rejected
item.description: Arsenal have gained a significant advantage in the Premier League title race after Manchester City's request to reschedule some fixtures was denied. City, who are three points behind Arsenal, face a t...

item.title: Saskatchewan man urges provincial action to improve rail safety following fatal train accidents
item.description: A widower in Saskatchewan is appealing to provincial authorities to take immediate action to improve rail safety at a location where multiple deadly train collisions have occurred. The man, who lost h...

item.title: Family discovers docket missing in fatal crash involving Ekurhuleni city manager
item.description: The family of Sandra Mathabathe Mamaila, who died in a car crash on May 14, 2021, involving Ekurhuleni city manager Kagiso Lerutla, has expressed shock after discovering that the police docket for the...
